abstract: Time is our most precious, nonrenewable resource, yet its mismanagement remains a critical barrier to addressing the global crises of climate change, economic inequality, and regional insecurity. While often overlooked, the squandering of time—not only our own but that of nature and others—destabilizes efforts to prevent violence and build sustainable peace. This article argues that we must radically overhaul our relationship with temporality to ensure collective well-being. By defining the nature of time and surveying current temporal deficiencies, the study proposes a framework for a more adaptive and responsible "temporament" specifically tailored to the field of conflict transformation.
